examples.title: Algorithm: Deadlock Detection (Banker's Approach)
examples.description: Detects deadlocks by checking if system can satisfy process requests
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DetectDeadlock
BEGIN
 // Initialize Work as available resources
 Work = Available.copy()
 
 // Initialize Finish array
 FOR each process i in 0..n-1:
 IF Allocation[i] == 0 for all resources:
 Finish[i] = true
 ELSE:
 Finish[i] = false
 
 // Find processes that can finish
 DO:
 found = false
 FOR each process i where Finish[i] == false:
 IF Request[i] <= Work:
 // Allocate resources and mark as finished
 Work += Allocation[i]
 Finish[i] = true
 found = true
 BREAK
 END FOR
 WHILE found
 
 // Check for deadlock
 IF all Finish[i] == true:
 RETURN "No Deadlock"
 ELSE:
 RETURN "Deadlock Detected"
END
examples.explanation:
1. Work initialized to available resources.
2. Finish[i] set based on current allocations.
3. Iteratively find processes that can complete with current Work.
4. If any process remains unfinished, deadlock exists.

examples.title: Algorithm: Cycle Detection in Wait-For Graph
examples.description: Uses DFS to detect cycles in single-instance resource systems
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DetectDeadlockCycle
BEGIN
 // For each node in the graph
 FOR each process p in processes:
 visited = empty set
 recursion_stack = empty set
 IF DFS(p, visited, recursion_stack) == true:
 RETURN "Deadlock Detected"
 END FOR
 RETURN "No Deadlock"
END

FUNCTION DFS(node, visited, recursion_stack)
BEGIN
 IF node not in visited:
 visited.add(node)
 recursion_stack.add(node)
 
 // Check all neighbors (processes this node is waiting for)
 FOR each neighbor in adjacency_list[node]:
 IF neighbor not in visited:
 IF DFS(neighbor, visited, recursion_stack) == true:
 RETURN true
 ELSE IF neighbor in recursion_stack:
 // Cycle detected
 RETURN true
 END FOR
 
 recursion_stack.remove(node)
 END IF
 RETURN false
END
examples.explanation:
1. Perform DFS traversal for each process.
2. Track recursion stack to detect back edges.
3. Cycle in recursion stack indicates deadlock.

examples.title: Algorithm: Recovery via Process Termination
examples.description: Breaks deadlock by terminating victim processes
examples.code:
ALGORITHM RecoverByTermination
BEGIN
 WHILE DetectDeadlock() == "Deadlock Detected":
 // Select victim based on criteria (e.g., lowest priority)
 victim = SelectVictimProcess()
 
 // Terminate and release resources
 TerminateProcess(victim)
 ReleaseResources(victim)
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation:
1. Continuously check for deadlock.
2. Select victim process using selection criteria.
3. Terminate victim and release its resources.
4. Repeat until deadlock is resolved.
